A vape pen with a Lithium Ion Battery overheated.  The pen was placed into a
Thermal Containment Bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A package containing Lithium Ion Batteries exploded and caught fire within a cargo facility.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A lithium ion battery stowed within a customers briefcase caught fire.  The fire took place within the aircraft cabin while positioned at the gate.  The battery was part of an e-cigarette.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A Lithium Battery heated up and expanded while in flight.  There was no release or fire reported.  The Battery was placed in a Thermal Containment Bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A disposable e-Cig fell into a lavatory and started to smoke.  The e-cig was placed in a Thermal Containment bag

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger's Vape Pen Battery started smoking onboard an aircraft while taxiing for takeoff.  The aircraft returned to the gate with no injuries reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passengers cell phone was smoking on-board the plane while travelling from Charlotte, NC to Frankfurt, Germany.  The phone was placed in a fire containment bag.  The flight was not diverted.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
During offloading, there was a Lithium Ion Battery found to be smoking and resulted
in a melted bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A Lithium Battery was discovered overheating and releasing smoke within the aircraft cabin.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: During the aviation cargo sortation process a hazardous materials package was discovered emitting smoke. Upon review of the package contents, nineteen Lithium- ion batteries had melted, possibly as a result of a handling error.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: An inflight thermal runaway event occurred when a passenger’s cell phone overheated and expanded. Aircrew personnel mitigated the incident by placing the phone inside a thermal containment bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger s checked bag burst into flames during the offloading process. The
suspected source of fire was a Lithium battery powered smart tag. Airport Fire

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: During sort operations, a Lithium Battery caught fire and released smoke inside of a building.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A thermal runaway event occurred during the boarding process. A Lithium battery inside a passenger’s carry-on baggage suddenly began to burn and emit smoke. The incident was contained by airline personnel. There were no injuries reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger's cell phone battery overheated while the aircraft was on its final approach to Denver, CO.  The incident was contained by airline personnel prior tolanding. There were no injuries reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger was charging a laptop computer with a small power bank.  The power bank started to smoke and spark in a thermal runaway event.  The incident was contained by airline personnel.  There were no injuries reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: An iPad went into thermal runaway during a flight from Korea to San Francisco International Airport over the Pacific Ocean. The incident was contained by airline personnel.  There were no injuries reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A laptop computer battery began to smoke while onboard a flight that was still at the gate.  The laptop was removed from the aircraft prior to takeoff.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger’s e-Cigarette started overheating.  The device was put in a battery containment bag.  No injuries were reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger was sleeping with a flashlight around their neck when the battery began to burn.  The flashlight was put in a “burn bag” and secured by airline personnel.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: An e-Cigarette device “sizzled and smoked” inflight while headed to Sacramento, CA from Seattle, WA.  Airline personnel contained the incident.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A piece of luggage containing a Lithium battery powered multi charger device was found smoking while being transferred to another flight. The luggage was removed from circulation by the airline.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger’s luggage was found damaged evidently by a small fire.    The heat
source was not determined but is suspected to be a Lithium battery.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger's cell phone battery began to smoke and was contained by airline personnel.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger’s e-Cigarette began smoking while in flight.  Airline personnel used a thermal containment bag to deescalate the incident.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
During routine screening, the Transportation Security Administration discovered a notebook computer with a swollen Lithium battery still hot. The
computer was removed from the luggage and the passenger was informed that the
computer could not be transported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A vaping device overheated while inflight from Los Cabos, Mexico to Chicago, IL. There was no release or injuries involved. The incident was contained by airline personnel.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) discovered a laptop with a swollen Lithium Battery, during the screening process.  The airline removed the laptop from the passenger’s luggage and the passenger was informed that the laptop could not be transported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A fire involving a Lithium battery inside of a package ensued at a cargo location.  The package contained four Lithium batteries of which one burned at the cargo
warehouse facility.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: While in flight, a battery charger overheated and released a small amount of smoke inside the aircraft.  The charging device was placed inside a thermal containment bag.  There were no injuries reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: During the deplaning process, an e-Cigarette Lithium battery started smoking. The passenger deplaned before airline personnel could assist.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A Lithium battery powered cell phone went into thermal runaway during flight. The fire was extinguished by airline personnel.  The cell phone was placed on a metal tray for temporary containment.  There were no injuries reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A Lithium battery powered Vape Pen went into thermal runaway and started sparking.  A flight attendant sprayed the vape device with a Halon extinguisher and placed it into a containment bag until the plane landed.  There were no injuries reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A Lithium battery inside of a package was found smoking due to unknown causes.
The incident occurred while the package was traveling down a conveyor belt.  No injuries were reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A shipment offered for air transportation was discovered smoking in cargo and isolated.  Item was determined that a Lithium Battery went into thermal runaway within the shipment.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: Reported that while aircraft was on the runway, a power bank started smoking. The power bank was placed inside a fire containment bag and aircraft returned to gate.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: Two passengers reportedly engaged in a tussle that resulted in a cell phone being damaged and produced smoke due to Lithium battery damage. As a result of the incident the aircraft was diverted to Pensacola International Airport.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: An aircraft passenger's phone started to smoke while on an aircraft at the Boston International Airport.  As a result, there was an emergency de-planement.  One passenger was injured during de-planement.  All passengers returned to gate area.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A pair of ear buds started smoking.  The ear buds were placed in a containment bag. The flight was in the process of pushing when this event occurred causing the flight to return to the gate.  Once at the gate, the ear buds were disposed.  There were no injuries or damage to the aircraft.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger's portable battery charging unit started to overheat and smoke.  This released smoke into the air onboard a flight.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A power bank in a passenger's back pack stored under seat started to overheat and smoke.  The incident occurred while onboard an airplane that was inflight.  There were no reported injuries.  Incident occurred 20 - 30 minutes prior to landing over
Tampa, Fl.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger's i-Phone 10 had a thermal discharge while the aircraft was at the gate.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A lithium battery charger that was in the luggage of a passenger exploded while
being transported from the gate to the aircraft.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger's vaping device began to overheat in his pocket while in the lavatory.
Flight attendants placed the device in a containment bag. Fire rescue met the aircrew upon landing and disposed of the device.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A package containing cellular phones and spare cell phone batteries was run over by handling equipment during cargo sortation causing a fire that was extinguished by
airport fire rescue upon arrival.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger’s cell phone battery began to burn while in flight over the Gulf of
Mexico.  Airline personnel extinguished the fire and secured the phone in a thermal containment bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger’s checked bag began to burn during routine screening operations at the TSA baggage inspection area.  The fire was contained by airport personnel.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger’s phone began to smoke while watching a movie on it in flight.  The phone was placed in an inflight containment bag until the flight landed.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A thermal event ensued when a pair of earbuds were being charged by a passenger
in flight.  The earbuds were placed in a thermal containment bag until the smoke was extinguished.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A package containing a laptop computer began to smolder when the battery overheated in a thermal runaway event.  The package was intercepted and placed in
a metal recovery drum.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A piece of checked luggage began to burn on the baggage claim belt.  The cause of fire was identified as an e-Cigarette charger that began to smolder and burn.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A portable electronic device heated and expanded while in flight.  Flight attendants
put the battery into a containment bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
An e-Cigarette caught fire in a passenger’s carry-on bag while the aircraft was still at
the gate.  The batteries were exposed to metal causing a short.  Two passengers sustained minor injuries.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A Lithium battery fire ensued when a package was being off loaded from a cargo
aircraft.  Fire department personnel arrived to extinguish the fire.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A shipment containing batteries began to burn inside of a package during the
handling process.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A shipment containing Lithium batteries began to burn inside of a unit load device causing an evacuation at an air cargo sort facility.  The incident was referred to the
USPS investigative service.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger was charging a power bank that began to overheat and smoke during flight.  The battery was extinguished and secured in a containment bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger's laptop computer began to smolder and burn while in-flight causing the
aircraft to divert and land at an alternate location.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger carry-on bag containing e-Cigarette batteries caught fire in the cabin.
Flight attendants quickly extinguished the blaze.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A Lithium battery used to power a portable Bluetooth speaker began to burn in a piece of luggage while in flight.  An inflight emergency was declared and the aircraft was diverted for an emergency landing.  Upon landing, the fire was contained.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: An e-cigarette charger began to burn inside a passenger's checked bag while being of loaded from the aircraft.  An airline employee sustained minor injuries.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: Two laptops containing batteries began to burn in a passengers checked baggage. No injuries were reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A package containing a Lithium-ion battery was dropped during the cargo handling process causing a fire.  The facility was evacuated as a precaution.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger alerted a flight attendant that their laptop screen began to melt during flight causing the pilot to divert and land at a different location.  The laptop was
placed in a fire containment bag and secured.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger’s vape pen battery started to burn at the landing gate.  The passenger reportedly immediately deplaned and fled.  Flight attendants used a fire extinguisher and containment bag to control the thermal event.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A driver noticed a burning smell coming from packages containing Lithium batteries
originating from overseas.  Charring and burn marks were discovered within one of the packages.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A shipment destined for air transport containing E-Cigarettes caught fire and while
loaded in a delivery van.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A portable electronic device (PED) began overheating and expanding during fight.
The PED was subsequently placed in a fire containment bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A battery powered welding device in checked baggage was left on by a passenger, was inadvertently activated and began smoking during offloading.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A pallet fire believed to have been caused by batteries erupted during transfer
sortation operations.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A vaping device began smoking while in checked luggage.  Officials contained the
incident and intercepted the device prior to loading.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger’s laptop began smoking in carry-on luggage during flight.  The laptop
was placed in a containment bag and monitored by crew.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
Two spare batteries began smoking in a passenger’s carry-on bag causing minor
damage to the aircraft carpet.  Passenger sustained minor burns.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: Two batteries in a passenger’s charger for a vape device started to smoke and melt.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
Three undeclared Lithium batteries concealed within a DVD player began smoking
during sortation operations.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
Passenger was using his laptop to charge batteries while in his carry-on prior to take off.  The batteries began to smoke causing the flight attendants to deplane the
aircraft to contain the fire.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A package containing a smart meter spontaneously combusted.  The fire was
extinguished and the package placed in a salvage drum.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
An e-Cigarette began smoking in a passenger’s pocket while using a laptop
computer to charge the device in flight.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger’s laptop battery began overheating in flight.  The battery was secured  in
a containment bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A flight was diverted when a passenger’s cell phone started to burn.  The cell phone
was placed in a containment bag and later discarded.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger traveling with a battery was burned when the battery caught fire shortly after takeoff burning the passenger’s shirt.  The fire was contained and the flight
continued on to its destination.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A lithium battery used to power an experimental aircraft exploded at Prescott
Airport in Prescott, AZ. The investigation is ongoing.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A Lithium battery began to smolder during sort operations.  Package was isolated
and contained in a salvage drum.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A vaping device began smoking while in a carry-on bag prior to entering a connecting
flight.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A vaping device began smoking during flight.  The matter was brought to the attention of flight attendants that contained the incident for the remainder of the
flight.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
At Indianapolis, IN, (IND) a passenger’s bag caught fire while it was on the baggage conveyor system en route to the baggage sortation room. An e-cigarette and spare
batteries were inside the bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A GoPro device overheated in the cabin while the preparing for take-off.  The Pilots
decided to return to the gate.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: On flight 1287, from NY, NY (LGA) to Atlanta, GA (ATL), a customer’s e-cigarette overheated. The device was placed in a battery containment bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: Upon arrival at St Croix, USVI, (STX), spare batteries from an e-cigarette fell from a passenger’s bag and caught fire when they impacted the floor, scorching the carpet.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A battery in a shipment was damaged during customs inspection and began to spark
and smoke. The battery was placed in a salvage drum.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
The battery of a passengers laptop overheated and expanded while being screened
at the TSA checkpoint in Sacramento, CA (SMF).

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: On flight 1932 from Las Vega, NV (LAS) to Detroit, MI (DTW), while taxiing to the gate upon arrival at DTW a fire occurred from a battery used for an electronic cigarette. On board fire containment bag was utilized to extinguish the fire.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
On flight 285, a lithium battery fire occurred during the boarding process. The on
board fire containment bag was utilized to extinguish the fire.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
UPS in Louisville, KY reported that a lithium-ion battery inside package experienced a dangerous evolution of heat that occurred. No fire occurred. The package was
charred and scorched.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: At the UPS facility in Cologne, Germany, a package was discovered emitting a burning odor; the package contained a lithium battery that was smoldering.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
In flight, about one hour away from Denver, CO, a passenger’s e cigarette with lithium batteries inside their coat began emitting a burning odor. Investigation is
ongoing.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: At the sort facility in Louisville, KY, while opening a box to perform an inspection, a lithium ion battery was drilled into resulting, in a thermal event (charring, smoldering / no flame).

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: On flight 2762 from Las Vegas, NV (LAS) to Chicago, IL (MDW) a passenger’s lithium battery began smoking during a flight. The flight attendant took the battery, placed it in a containment bag and cooled it with water.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: While a passenger was boarding a flight their bag began emitting smoke. The bag contained an electronic cigarette, the battery and its charger was burned.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: In Dallas, TX (DFW), a passenger bag containing a portable speaker caught fire in a baggage cart on the ramp at gate D34 . The fire was extinguished, but several adjacent passenger bags were affected as well. All baggage involved was placed in American Airlines hazmat holding for further investigation.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
While deplaning, a passenger's laptop was smoking. The passenger ran onto the boarding bridge with the laptop. No further information about the type of battery or
the passenger's name.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A package containing an I-phone was dropped and dragged by handling equipment and subsequently caught fire. An employee submersed the package in water to
extinguish the fire.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
During shipment handling in the cargo warehouse, a forklift operator inadvertently punctured a box on a pallet. The box caught fire when the forklift blade made contact with the lithium batteries inside. The fire only lasted a few seconds and self
extinguished.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: On a SkyWest flight from San Francisco, CA (SFO) to Austin, TX (AUS) a passenger’s portable battery charger began to heat up and would not turn off, it then began to smoke. The flight attendant placed the device in a battery containment bag and monitored the device.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: During baggage loading, a customer’s checked bag began emitting smoke while it was on the baggage cart prior to being loaded on the aircraft. The source was identified as spare lithium batteries contained within portable charger. Batteries shorted out, began to burn and caused clothing contained within to smolder and melt. The bag was extinguished and returned to TSA for additional screening. No injuries or additional damage was reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A passenger's cell phone began smoking during boarding. The phone was placed in a
fire containment bag with water and removed from aircraft. No injuries or damage reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A package was discovered smoldering during the sorting process at the Erlanger, KY (CVG) HUB. The package was removed from the sort system and placed in a salvage drum. It contained electronic equipment with a lithium-ion battery, approximately 50wh. The inner package showed signs of burning.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
At JFK Airport, a Unit Load Device (ULD) was inadvertently dropped as it was being transported from the warehouse to the tarmac. Shortly after it a package inside of it began to emit smoke and soon after was on fire. The ULD contained several boxes of
used laptops

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
After Southwest airlines flight 1903 was fully boarded, a passenger’s carry-on bag under the seat, containing and e-cig nit core i4 battery charger with two batteries started to smoke. The flight attendant used the halon fire extinguisher. The aircraft
was evacuated and taken out of service.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: While going through TSA screening, a passenger’s carry on bag caught fire and was put out by a fire extinguisher. The cause of the fire appeared to have come from items in a pouch, which included a Power Bank.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A passenger’s phone became lodged in their seat while they were sleeping, they awoke to the phone smoking. The phone was placed in a containment bag and then returned to the passenger upon arrival in Houston.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: Approximately 7 hours into flight AA731 from London, UK (LHR) to Charlotte, NC (CLT), a bag in the overhead compartment began to smoke. It was removed and extinguished. The source appeared to be an e-cigarette which was placed in a containment bag. No other damage was reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: Onboard DL flight 68, from Narita, Japan (NRT) to Portland, OR (PWM) a battery pack plugged into a passengers tablet caught fire. It was extinguished and placed in a containment bag. No other damage was reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: A large lithium-ion battery (1,200 Wh) caught fire at a cargo warehouse in Los Angeles, CA (LAX). The fire was extinguished but then re-ignited several times. It was being shipped as US Mail and was not declared to contain any hazardous material.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: While loading baggage on DL flight 880 from Baltimore, MD (BWI) to Minneapolis, MN (MSP), ramp personnel noticed a bag on the belt loader emitting smoke. It was placed onto the ground and caught fire. The fire department was called and they extinguished the bag. The bag contained an E-cigarette, spare batteries.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
A package containing a power pack/charger was run over and dragged by a piece of
ground equipment and subsequently ignited. It was extinguished and held in a salvage drum for inspection.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
On PSA flight 5062, from Sarasota, FL (SRQ) to Charlotte, NC (CLT) a passenger s
laptop began to emit signs of a dangerous evolution of heat while onboard, prior to pushback. The laptop was thrown out of the aircraft by the flight attendant, where the ramp personnel used a fire extinguisher to prevent the laptop from actually

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: While waiting to board her flight, a passenger’s E-cigarette began to smolder and emit smoke while she was holding it. She dropped the device and it began to make popping sounds. The fire department responded and rendered the device safe. No injuries or damage were reported.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
On SWIA flight 5598 from Los Angeles (LAX) to Mammoth, CA (MMH), after push back, a flight attendant's electronic flight attendant device (EFAD) battery caught on fire. The fire was extinguished quickly and the EFAD was placed in the fire
containment bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: Onboard flight 3946, from Austin, TX (AUS) to Kansas City, KS (MCI), a passenger’s laptop began to spark and smoke.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
On a flight from Chicago, IL (ORD) to London, UK (LHR), a passenger reported that a cell phone began to smoke after it was inadvertently lodged between two seats.
Investigation is ongoing. No further information available at this time

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: On a flight from Manchester, UK (MAN) to Newark, NJ (EWR), a passenger reported that an e-cigarette began to smoke. The item was placed into a containment bag. Investigation is ongoing. No further information available at this time.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
In St. Kitts (SKB), during baggage loading, a passenger’s checked bag was found to be emitting smoke and flames in the cargo bin. Airline personnel extinguished the fire. The bag contained an E-cigarette battery charger and spare lithium-ion batteries.
The floor of the aircraft bin was scorched and burned. The aircraft was taken out of service for the day while the cleanup was completed and damage was assessed.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: During passenger security screening at the TSA checkpoint, a passenger’s tablet overheated and began to smoke. The tablet was removed from the screening area by the Port Authority Police and is being held at the Port Authority Police Station. No other damage or injuries were reported. The item was a Samsung Tablet.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
During flight, approximately :45 minutes prior to arrival into Cleveland, OH (CLE), a passenger gave the Flight Attendant a cell phone battery charger with flashlight that was hot and began to smoke. The device was placed in the on-board fire
containment bag.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description: In San Diego, CA (SAN), during baggage loading, a passenger’s checked bag was found to be emitting smoke in the cargo bin. The bag was removed and extinguished by Southwest Airlines personnel. The bag contained an E-cigarette battery charger and spare lithium-ion batteries. Other bags and packages adjacent to the bag were damaged. The aircraft was damaged and taken out of service.

Text...Source: Federal Aviation Administration, Security and Hazardous Materials Safety
Reported.Description:
In Chicago, IL (MDW), a passenger’s Li-ion Battery Power Bank (Capacity 8000 mAh
3.7V (29.6Wh)) began to smoking prior to pushback. The power bank removed and given to Operations staff.

their cell phone and it started smoking. The cellphone was placed in a fire containment bag and the smoke stopped.
